title: macOS SSH agent forwarding not working (with YubiKey)
body:
### Issue Description

Forwarding the SSH agent from macOS to a Podman container gives an "Operation not supported" error on the SSH agent socket.  We have a requirement to use PKCS#11 hardware for SSH keys (YubiKey), so this blocks using Podman containers for SSH access (e.g. git)

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run `podman run --rm -ti -v $SSH_AUTH_SOCK:$SSH_AUTH_SOCK -e SSH_AUTH_SOCK=$SSH_AUTH_SOCK registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/go-toolset bash`
2. Observe error: `Error: statfs /private/tmp/com.apple.launchd.30btFozyon/Listeners: operation not supported`

### Describe the results you received

```
$ echo $SSH_AUTH_SOCK 
/private/tmp/com.apple.launchd.30btFozyon/Listeners

$ ls -l $SSH_AUTH_SOCK
srw-rw-rw-  1 arthurbarr  wheel  0  7 Jul 14:42 /private/tmp/com.apple.launchd.30btFozyon/Listeners

$ podman machine ssh ls -l $SSH_AUTH_SOCK
ls: cannot access '/private/tmp/com.apple.launchd.30btFozyon/Listeners': Operation not supported

$ podman machine ssh ls -lZ /private/tmp/com.apple.launchd.30btFozyon/
ls: /private/tmp/com.apple.launchd.30btFozyon/Listeners: No such file or directory
total 0
srw-rw-rw-. 1 core core system_u:object_r:nfs_t:s0 0 Jul  7 14:42 Listeners

 $ podman machine ssh stat -f $SSH_AUTH_SOCK                              
stat: cannot read file system information for '/private/tmp/com.apple.launchd.30btFozyon/Listeners': Operation not supported
```

### Describe the results you expected

Mount the socket and be able to use SSH agent.

### podman info output

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

macOS Sonoma 14.5

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: I don't think virtiofs can share sockets so you need to another way to share the socket into the VM.
OK, thanks for the info.  I guess this can be closed then, if it's not going to be possible.  I'd still really like to find a way to do this.
Feel free to file a RFE for exposing the ssh agent to the machine.
